steps.title: Read the blank's job
steps.content: Both exposure conditions (ship sounds alone; ship sounds + sonic pulses) produced nearly identical diving behavior — the blank must explain why adding sonic pulses on top of ship sounds changed nothing. The key phrase is “differences between diving behaviors in the two exposure types were negligible.”

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — narwhals forage at shallower depths in the presence of ship sounds alone than in the presence of ship sounds coupled with sonic pulses.: This says diving did differ between the two exposure types — directly contradicting “negligible differences.” ✗
  • Choice B — sonic pulses can be heard at significantly greater ocean depths than ship sounds can.: Depth of sound propagation doesn’t explain why the narwhals reacted identically to both conditions. ✗
  • Choice C — the narwhals weren't as sensitive to human-caused sounds as the researchers had predicted.: Both conditions caused significant decreases in deep dives, proving the narwhals clearly reacted to noise — sensitivity is not in question. ✗
  • Choice D — ship sounds contribute so much to the overall sound level that the addition of sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals' auditory environment.: If ship sounds already dominate the acoustic environment, adding sonic pulses produces no meaningful change — perfectly explaining negligible behavioral differences. ✓
D is correct.
